prompt:
You are Agent 9 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-veggfU.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Update user notification preferences to support multiple channels (email + SMS + webhook).

Specifically:
1. Check coordination context — the previous session (Session 8) added SMS support and flagged that user preferences need updating.
2. Review the current user preferences model and service from Session 3.
3. The original preferences assumed email-only. This assumption is no longer valid — SMS was added in Session 8.
4. Refactor the preferences model to support per-channel settings (email, SMS, webhook).
5. Update the preferences service to handle multi-channel preferences.
6. Update the notification service to check per-channel preferences before sending.
7. Update tests to cover multi-channel scenarios.
8. Make sure the codebase compiles and all tests pass.

Important:
- This is a conscious decision change — the email-only assumption from Session 3 is explicitly invalidated.
- Preserve backward compatibility where possible (existing email preferences should still work).
- Document the decision change and why.

parameters.task: Update user notification preferences to support multiple channels (email + SMS + webhook). Refactor preferences model from email-only to per-channel settings.

alternatives.option: Add separate shouldSendSms() and shouldSendWebhook() methods per channel
alternatives.reason_rejected: Doesn't scale — each new channel would require a new method. Generic shouldSend(userId, type, channel) handles all channels uniformly and is extensible.

alternatives.option: Flatten preferences into a single map of channel → settings instead of named fields
alternatives.reason_rejected: Loses type safety for channel-specific settings (email has frequency, others don't). Named fields with typed interfaces are safer and more discoverable.

assumptions: All channels share the same base preference structure (enabled + subscribedCategories)
Channels without explicit preference support should default to allowed
Default preferences enable all channels for new users
parameters.context: Session 8 added SMS support and flagged that user preferences (designed email-only in Session 3) need updating. With email, SMS, and webhook adapters all present, the email-only preference model was no longer valid. SMS and webhook notifications were bypassing preference checks entirely.
parameters.rationale: Extended UserNotificationPreferences with per-channel settings (sms, webhook fields alongside existing email). Added ChannelNotificationSettings base interface. Created generic shouldSend(userId, type, channel) in PreferencesService, kept shouldSendEmail() as backward-compatible delegate. Updated NotificationService to check preferences for ALL channels, not just email. Default preferences enable all channels with all categories. Channels without explicit preference support (e.g., in-app) default to allowed.
parameters.summary: Refactor user preferences from email-only to per-channel settings (email, SMS, webhook) with generic shouldSend() method

parameters.reason: Resolved by agent-9: preferences model now supports email, SMS, and webhook channels with per-channel settings

parameters.detail:
Refactored user notification preferences from email-only to multi-channel support:

## Files modified:
- `src/models/user-preferences.ts` — Added `ChannelNotificationSettings` base interface, `SmsNotificationSettings`, `WebhookNotificationSettings`. Extended `UserNotificationPreferences` with `sms` and `webhook` fields. Updated `UpdatePreferencesInput` for all channels. Added `getChannelSettings()` helper. `getDefaultPreferences()` now returns defaults for all 3 channels (all enabled, all categories).
- `src/services/preferences.service.ts` — Added generic `shouldSend(userId, type, channel)` method that works for all channels. Kept `shouldSendEmail()` as backward-compatible delegate. Updated `updatePreferences()` to handle sms/webhook input. Extracted `clonePreferences()` helper for defensive copying across all channels.
- `src/services/notification.service.ts` — Changed preference check from email-only (`channel === 'email'` + `shouldSendEmail`) to all-channel (`shouldSend` with channel param). Now SMS and webhook notifications are properly preference-gated.
- `src/index.ts` — Exports new types: `ChannelNotificationSettings`, `SmsNotificationSettings`, `WebhookNotificationSettings`, `getChannelSettings`.
- `tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts` — Added 14 new tests for multi-channel: default SMS/webhook prefs, SMS enable/disable, SMS categories, webhook enable/disable, webhook categories, multi-channel update, independent channel checks, in-app fallback, reset across channels.

## Decision change:
Supersedes decision 01KMYCZJVPPY7X3X5PJAJ87NSH (email-only preferences). The assumption "email is the only channel for now" is no longer valid with SMS and webhook adapters in place.

## All 181 tests pass. TypeScript compiles cleanly.
parameters.summary: Agent-9: Multi-channel preferences complete — email/SMS/webhook per-channel settings, generic shouldSend(), all 181 tests passing
